summary: Small game-mode decomp matching cleanup across gm_1601, gm_16AE, gm_16F1, and gmmain_lib. The PR tightened several function prototypes to narrower or more semantically accurate types, introduced a static inline helper for repeated spawn-point lookup logic, rewrote some expressions to better match code generation, removed now-unneeded static BSS declarations, and changed gmMainLib_8015DADC from s32 to bool to reflect predicate usage.
what_changed: Updated fn_801695BC and fn_8016989C signatures in gm_1601.c and gm_1601.h so the second and third parameters use u8 instead of s32/long-style wider types.
Removed an explicit cast to s32 when passing Player_GetCostumeId output into fn_8016989C, consistent with the new u8 prototype.
Changed a counter condition in fn_8016D634 from pre-increment to post-increment: ++lbl_8046B6A0.unk_30 became lbl_8046B6A0.unk_30++.
Added static inline getSpawnPoint(int i, Vec3* v) in gm_16AE.c to centralize repeated logic selecting either the player index or FighterMatchInfo[i].spawn_point before calling Stage_80224E64.
Replaced several duplicated spawn-point lookup blocks in fn_8016DEEC, fn_8016E2BC, and gm_8016EDDC with getSpawnPoint calls.
Removed a temporary s8 variable in gm_8016EDDC after the spawn-point helper made it unnecessary.
Changed fn_8016FAD4 kind parameter from u32 to int in gm_16F1.c and gm_16F1.h.
Initialized rankings in fn_8016FAD4 with u8 rankings[7] = { 0 } instead of manually loading lbl_804DA2E8, lbl_804DA2EC, and lbl_804DA2EE into the byte array.
Removed static declarations lbl_804DA2E8, lbl_804DA2EC, and lbl_804DA2EE from gm_16F1.static.h after the rankings initialization no longer referenced them.
Rewrote gm_80173EEC_inline filtering logic from one compound condition into a sequence of early continue checks followed by if (!gmMainLib_8015DADC(i)).
Changed gmMainLib_8015DADC return type from s32 to bool in gmmain_lib.c and gmmain_lib.h.
smart_moves: Used a static inline helper for spawn-point selection instead of duplicating the same if spawn_point == -1 pattern in multiple functions; this preserved local codegen control while reducing repeated source shapes.
Narrowed argument types where call sites and data usage indicate byte-sized values, which can affect register extension behavior and improve matching.
Converted a bitset query function to bool, matching how callers use it as a predicate and enabling simpler boolean-style source in gm_80173EEC_inline.
Replaced opaque manual BSS-backed initialization of rankings with an explicit zero initializer, eliminating unused static symbols and making the intended initial state clearer.
Split a dense exclusion predicate into ordered continue statements, a common matching tactic when compiler branch layout differs from an equivalent combined boolean expression.
decomp_lessons: For decomp matching, equivalent C expressions are not interchangeable: pre-increment versus post-increment can materially change generated code.
When a function parameter semantically holds IDs or small enum-like values, using u8 instead of s32 may be necessary for correct extension/truncation and matching.
Static inline helpers can be useful in matching work when repeated logic must have the same emitted shape across several call sites without introducing an out-of-line function.
Manual reconstruction of small arrays from adjacent BSS symbols may be avoidable; a local aggregate initializer can both simplify source and eliminate questionable symbol declarations.
Predicate functions that return bit-test results should use bool when the source is consumed as true/false, but the change must be coordinated between .c and .h declarations.
Large compound boolean exclusions can be more match-friendly when expressed as sequential early exits or continues, especially inside loops.
naming_conventions: Preserved existing decomp placeholder function names such as fn_801695BC, fn_8016989C, fn_8016FAD4, and gm_80173EEC_inline where better semantic names were not evidenced.
New helper was named getSpawnPoint, using lower camel case and directly describing the repeated Stage_80224E64 spawn-point lookup behavior.
Existing project type names and globals were retained, including lbl_8046B6A0_t, FighterMatchInfo, PlayerInitData, Vec3, and Gm_PKind_NA.
Header prototypes were kept synchronized with implementation type changes in gm_1601.h, gm_16F1.h, and gmmain_lib.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed pre-increment to post-increment in fn_8016D634 to alter side-effect timing and likely match the target instruction sequence.
Used narrower u8 parameters for fn_801695BC and fn_8016989C to influence argument conversion and register value handling.
Used a static inline getSpawnPoint helper to coax repeated spawn-point selection code into a consistent inlined form.
Removed an unnecessary temporary variable in gm_8016EDDC after helper extraction, reducing stack/register pressure.
Initialized u8 rankings[7] with { 0 } instead of writing s32/s16/s8 chunks through casts, changing local initialization codegen and removing dependence on lbl_804DA2E8/lbl_804DA2EC/lbl_804DA2EE.
Rewrote gm_80173EEC_inline's compound if into multiple continue checks, likely to match branch ordering and short-circuit structure.
Changed gmMainLib_8015DADC to bool and used !gmMainLib_8015DADC(i), aligning source predicate form with boolean branch generation.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR context.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but the excerpt is empty, so no specific reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename getSpawnPoint or further type FighterMatchInfo.spawn_point.
Search for callers of fn_801695BC and fn_8016989C to confirm whether u8 parameter types propagated cleanly.
Check whether gmMainLib_8015DADC returning bool affected any non-boolean callers or comparisons.
Look for follow-up cleanup around lbl_804DA2F0/lbl_804DA2F4/lbl_804DA2F6 to see if the neighboring static symbols were similarly replaced by local initializers.
Search for other game-mode spawn-point lookup duplicates that could reuse the getSpawnPoint pattern.
